summary: Every load-bearing primitive in Bead 2's plan exists in the real code and the 6-step order is implementable without a half-state. (1) WorktreeOps interface (internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go L27-31) has Remove(name string)/Create/List; it is the exported DI field on *MindspecExecutor (L46-53), so release can call exec.WorktreeOps.Remove directly. next.CheckDirtyTree(repoRoot, cwd) exists (internal/next/guard.go L110) returning (userDirt []string, err) — usable for the refuse-unless-force pre-gate. (2) The os.Chdir(root) pattern is real (internal/complete/complete.go L576), placed AFTER worktree removal (exec.CompleteBead L560) and BEFORE the bd subprocesses — exactly the mechanism the plan mirrors; root is resolved at the cmd layer via findRoot() (cmd/mindspec/complete.go L41) and CompleteBead itself uses withWorkingDir(g.Root, ...) (executor L749, L250). (3) Worktree path/name is resolvable via public helpers: workspace.BeadWorktreeName(beadID) for Remove and workspace.BeadWorktreePath(specWorktree,cfg,beadID) for the dirty-check; CompleteBead's own find-loop (List + match name/branch, L182-189) is reusable pattern. (4) bd-state mutation: bd runs embedded auto-commit (complete.go honesty-clause L66-77), so `bd update <id> --status=open` clear-assignee via bead.RunBDCombined persists with no MINDSPEC_ALLOW_MAIN flag needed — that env var is only a git pre-commit-hook escape hatch (hook/dispatch.go L51), not a bd passthrough; plan correctly places the mutation in release.go/internal/release, OUT of beads.go. (5) Cursor is genuinely DERIVED from bd's single in_progress child (phase.FindActiveBeadForEpic, derive.go L709), so set-open self-rewinds the derived cursor; the explicit mindspec_phase cache sync the plan's step 6 cites is the real complete.go step 6.5 (L684-691, completeMergeMetadataFn epic {mindspec_phase: nextMode}). (6) bead.WorktreeRemove IS hardcoded --force at the bd-CLI layer (bead/bdcli.go L203), confirming the plan's mandate that mindspec --force must be a pre-gate, not a passthrough. The remove-first/set-open-last ordering leaves only the recoverable still-claimed-worktree-gone state on partial failure. Test seams (newRepoExecutor executor_test.go L112, TestWithWorkingDir_RemovedCwdRemainsAtDirSilently L994, fakeWorktreeOps, runBDCombFn/runBDFn) all exist as cited. No missing primitive, no wrong chdir mechanism, no unimplementable step.
